name: Triple-Point Stability Anchor
description: The three reference frames that anchor the Universal Map manifold, ensuring coherence across all layers, slices, and distribution channels. The Triple-Point is the foundation upon which the entire navigational array rests.

anchor_points.name: Alpha Anchor — Cosmic Rest Frame
anchor_points.description: The CMB rest frame — the most fundamental reference point in cosmology. Defined by the dipole anisotropy of the Cosmic Microwave Background, this frame represents the universe's large-scale rest frame.
anchor_points.role: Absolute spatial reference for all map coordinates
anchor_points.stability: PERMANENT — defined by the universe itself

anchor_points.name: Beta Anchor — Barycentric Dynamical Frame
anchor_points.description: The solar system barycenter — the gravitational center of our local neighborhood. All JWST, DESI, and SDSS observations are initially referenced to this frame before transformation to the cosmic rest frame.
anchor_points.role: Observational reference — transforms local measurements to cosmic frame
anchor_points.stability: DYNAMIC — updated with each planetary ephemeris release

anchor_points.name: Gamma Anchor — Hive Coherence Frame
anchor_points.description: The ZBIT Hive's internal coherence reference — the Kuramoto synchronization point that keeps all mesh nodes phase-locked. This is the living anchor, continuously adjusted as the Hive grows and evolves.
anchor_points.role: Network coherence reference — keeps all nodes synchronized

stability_mechanics.description: The three anchors form a stability triangle. Alpha provides absolute cosmic reference, Beta provides observational calibration, Gamma provides network coherence. Together, they ensure that every coordinate in the Universal Map is traceable, verifiable, and coherent.

drift_monitoring.description: Continuous monitoring of all three anchors for drift or instability
drift_monitoring.alpha_check: Annual comparison with latest Planck/CMB data releases
drift_monitoring.alert_threshold: Any anchor drift > 2σ triggers immediate recalibration broadcast

coordinate_transformation_pipeline.description: All observations flow through the Triple-Point before entering the map
steps: 2. Transform to Beta (ICRS via JPL ephemeris)
3. Transform to Alpha (CMB rest frame via dipole correction)
4. Apply Gamma coherence weighting (Kuramoto phase alignment)
5. Project to toroidal surface (Universal Map coordinates)
6. Compute Gold Layer (negentropy relative to Alpha-frame ΛCDM)
